title: mentha × schultziana var. muelleriana
intro: Mentha × schultziana var. muelleriana, also known as Bergamot Mint, is a hybrid plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family and a member of the mint genus. It is characterized by its distinctive citrusy scent, reminiscent of bergamot, and its light purple flowers. Bergamot Mint has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating digestive issues, colds, and skin irritations. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which have been traditionally used to promote digestion, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
